summary: Computed path.resolve/path.join consumers and generated TypeScript output extend the direct-reference closure.
Backend npm scripts and four mode-corpus node test commands are confirmed; styles engine/database commands remain inferred until baseline execution confirms them.
Name-only cutover requires byte and observable parity, exact-token classification, and one atomic reverse patch.
Normalization, projector bridge, semantic parity, v2 writer/hash switch, and bridge deletion are separate ordered gates.
Versioned generation identities may change; deterministic recomputation and semantic output parity are the valid proof.
Formatting/order, acquisition, artifact, and poisoned-derived-payload mutations test distinct invalidation contracts.
Bridge deletion requires zero live readers and clean rebuilds; mutable current docs update while frozen history remains verbatim.
Iteration 5 must be a critical consistency/adversarial pass over iterations 1-4, not synthesis.
